name: Carlos -- Reliability Manager
role: Reliability Manager at a Gulf Coast refinery. Mechanical engineer with 15 years in rotating equipment. Reports to the Plant Manager. Manages 2 reliability engineers.
goals: Prevent unplanned failures before they happen by catching degradation trends early
Eliminate the top 10 bad actors by cost and failure frequency this year
Improve the planned vs unplanned maintenance ratio from 72% to 85%+
Build a data-driven case for capital replacement of chronically failing assets
frustrations: Vibration alerts fire but nobody acts on them until the asset trips. Three days of warnings on K-101 and it still tripped.
Alarm thresholds are set wrong. Some too high (miss real problems), some too low (cry wolf). No systematic way to review and calibrate them.
Work order closeout data is garbage. Techs write 'fixed pump' with no failure codes. Can't do MTBF analysis on bad data.
Spends 30 minutes every morning piecing together what happened overnight from three different systems (APM, CMMS, email).
RCA investigations take months because corrective actions stall in the work order backlog.
The dashboard shows current state but not the trend. A number without 'compared to what' is useless.
behaviors: First thing every morning: checks overnight alarms and condition monitoring alerts
Reviews bad actor list weekly, updates it monthly
Opens cases for any failure on a criticality A or B asset
Reads the dashboard BACKWARD in time: starts at the failure, traces what led to it
Compares KPI trends across 90-day, 6-month, and annual windows
Correlates KPI drops with specific asset events to find the cause
Creates asset strategies that change how maintenance is done on specific equipment
Presents unplanned downtime and bad actor progress to plant leadership monthly
tools_used: APM platform (asset health, condition monitoring, RCA, asset strategy)
CMMS/EAM (reads work order history for failure analysis, does not manage work orders)
Excel/Power BI for reporting to leadership
NotebookLM or similar for organizing RCA findings
time_horizon: 90 days to years. Strategic. Looking at patterns not just today's problems.
core_question: Why does this keep failing, and how do I eliminate the failure mode permanently?
quote: I had three days of vibration alerts on K-101. Three days. And it still tripped at 2 AM because the alarm was set at 7.1 mm/s, which is basically waiting for the trip. If I had an alert at 4.5 I would have caught it on Wednesday and done a planned shutdown instead.
